llcFilesWith: Louisiana Secretary of State (Application to Register Trade Name, Trademark, or Service Mark, Form 309), filed through the GeauxBiz portal
soleProprietorFilesWith: Louisiana Secretary of State as well; individuals and partnerships register the same trade name centrally, and Louisiana has no county-level DBA filing
feeBasis: The $75 Secretary of State fee registers a trade name for ten years for any filer, entity or individual.
renewalRule: Renewable for successive ten-year terms by filing a renewal before the registration expires.
protectionNote: A trade name registration is a notice filing; exclusive rights in a name come from a registered trademark or service mark, not from the trade name registration. It carries a practical effect at the state level: because § 1306 screens new entity names against registered trade names, a registered trade name blocks a later corporation, LLC, or partnership name that is not distinguishable from it while active.
searchNote: Trade names registered with the Secretary of State appear in the same Commercial Division records search as entities, since they are part of the § 1306 distinguishability records. Louisiana keeps DBA filings entirely at the state level, so there is no separate parish (county) index to check.
notes: Louisiana runs a clean state-level DBA system: every filer, whether an LLC, corporation, sole proprietor, or partnership, registers a trade name with the Secretary of State on Form 309 for $75, good for ten years and renewable. There is no newspaper-publication requirement and no parish-level filing. Because registered trade names sit in the same records the state screens for distinguishability, a trade name behaves defensively like an entity name while active, though registration alone grants no trademark right.

sources.title: Louisiana Trademarks and Trade Names — registration with the Secretary of State
